question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit findById() to retrieve a ContractResult with a gasLimit that is exactly equal to the EVM's maximum gas per block (15 million), causing the throttle manager to consume the entire block gas budget in a single transaction and denying service to all other users in that block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit findById() to retrieve a ContractResult with a gasConsumed value that is exactly 1 unit less than gasLimit, causing the fee system to compute a gas refund of 1 unit, which when multiplied by gasPrice results in a near-zero refund that effectively charges the full gasLimit as fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit findById() to retrieve a ContractResult with a gasUsed value that is exactly equal to the estimateGasIterationThresholdPercent of gasLimit, causing the BinaryGasEstimator to terminate the binary search at the threshold boundary and return a gas estimate that is exactly at the minimum acceptable fee level?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit findById() to retrieve a ContractResult with a gasConsumed value that is exactly equal to the intrinsic gas cost (21000), causing the fee system to charge only the minimum intrinsic fee regardless of the actual contract execution cost?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit findById() to retrieve a ContractResult with a gasLimit that is exactly equal to the gasUsed from a previous successful transaction, causing the BinaryGasEstimator to use this as the lower bound and return a gas estimate that is exactly equal to the minimum required gas, resulting in the lowest possible fee?
